prompt:
You are identifying a stable interaction map for an application UI.

Target app: Pages
Image size: 1031x883
Grid size: 120px

Tasks:
1. Identify visible interactable elements that a user could click.
2. For each element, return its id, label, aliases, absolute click point x/y, layout mode, and action.
3. Propose one stable primary anchor and 2-3 alternate primary anchor candidates.
4. Propose an optional secondary anchor only when needed, plus 1-2 alternate secondary anchor candidates.
5. For each anchor, return a crop_box with absolute x/y/width/height.
6. If both window-level controls and content tiles are visible, prefer window-level controls, title bars, mode switchers, toolbar icons, or other persistent chrome as anchors. Do not use content tiles as anchors unless there is no better stable choice.
7. Anchor ids and element ids must be semantic snake_case names, not grid IDs.
8. Prefer anchors that are visually unique, tight crops, and likely to remain stable while the app is resized. Prefer window chrome, logos, toolbar icons, or other fixed visuals over content tiles or repeated buttons.
9. Anchor crop_box values must be tight around a single distinctive visual region, not a full-width toolbar or title bar. In most cases an anchor should be far smaller than 20 percent of the image width and 15 percent of the image height.
10. Use only these layout values: fixed_from_primary, top_right, bottom_right, center_scaled.
11. Use only click as the action.
12. Set secondary_anchor to null unless at least one element uses top_right, bottom_right, or center_scaled.
13. A bad anchor causes the profile to be rejected, so choose the tightest stable anchors you can.

Return JSON matching the provided schema and do not wrap it in markdown.
